name: imagen
description: Generates images using Google Gemini's image generation model for frontend UIs, documentation, and visual representations.
capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Google Gemini Pro Image model
Save generated images to custom file paths and directories
Specify image output size (supports options like 2K resolution)
Create images for UI components, documentation, and visual content
Works cross-platform on Windows, macOS, and Linux
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation file (SKILL.md) describing an image generation tool. All static findings are false positives: (1) Markdown code fences were misidentified as Ruby backtick execution, (2) The GitHub source URL is legitimate metadata, (3) Documenting GEMINI_API_KEY as an environment variable is the correct secure practice. No actual security risks identified.
valueStatement: Create stunning images directly in your Claude Code workflow using Google Gemini AI. Perfect for frontend development, documentation visuals, and creative projects without leaving your terminal.
limitations: Requires a Google Gemini API key to function
Depends on Gemini API availability and rate limits

useCases.title: Frontend Developer Hero Images
useCases.description: Quickly generate hero images and visual assets for landing pages and web applications without needing external design tools.

useCases.targetUser: Technical writers and developers documenting projects
useCases.title: Documentation Illustration
useCases.description: Create diagrams and illustrations for README files, technical documentation, and project wikis.

useCases.targetUser: UI/UX designers and developers creating mockups
useCases.title: UI Asset Creation
useCases.description: Generate placeholder images, icons, and UI assets for component development and prototyping.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Image Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate an image of [describe your image concept] and save it to ./generated/image.png
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a simple image created from a text description

promptTemplates.title: High Resolution Image
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a high resolution (2K) image of [describe image] and save it to [path]
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a high-quality image for print or large displays

promptTemplates.title: UI Component Asset
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a [type: icon/avatar/placeholder] image for a [describe component context] and save it to [desired path]
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating specific UI assets for web or mobile applications

promptTemplates.title: Documentation Diagram
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a [style: diagram/illustration/chart] showing [concept to visualize] for technical documentation and save to [path]
promptTemplates.scenario: Visualizing architectures, flows, or concepts for documentation

bestPractices: Set your GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable before running the skill
Use descriptive, detailed prompts for better image results
Specify output paths that match your project structure
antiPatterns: Do not share your GEMINI_API_KEY in code or public repositories
Avoid generating extremely large images as they may timeout
Do not rely on this for critical production assets without backup plans

faq.question: How do I get a Gemini API key?
faq.answer: Visit the Google AI Studio or Google Cloud Console to obtain a Gemini API key. Set it as the GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable before using the skill.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: Currently only PNG format is supported for generated images.

faq.question: Can I generate multiple images at once?
faq.answer: The skill processes one image at a time. For batch generation, you would need to run the command multiple times or modify the script.

faq.question: Is this skill free to use?
faq.answer: The skill itself is free, but you pay for Google Gemini API usage based on your API key's associated billing account.

faq.question: Why is my image generation failing?
faq.answer: Common causes include: invalid or missing GEMINI_API_KEY, API rate limits, network issues, or prompts that violate Gemini's content policies.

faq.question: Can I use this for commercial projects?
faq.answer: Yes, generated images can be used in commercial projects, but check Gemini's terms of service for any restrictions on generated content usage.
